explanation: This command uses the 'sc.exe sdset' functionality to set a service's security descriptor with a DACL that grants access to the AU (Authenticated Users) trustee. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by including 'sdset', 'A;' (Allow Access), and ';AU' in the command line.

This command is a direct match to the Sigma rule. It uses 'sc.exe sdset' with an 'A;' (Allow Access) DACL and ';AU' (Authenticated Users) as the trustee, which are both key indicators in the rule's detection logic.
This command sets a DACL granting Generic All (GA) access to the WD (Everyone) trustee. It includes 'sdset', 'A;' (Allow Access), and ';WD' (Everyone), which are all detected by the Sigma rule.
This command grants access to the IU (Interactively logged-on user) trustee. It includes 'sdset', 'A;' (Allow Access), and ';IU', which are all key elements in the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command sets a DACL granting access to the SY (Local System) trustee. It includes 'sdset', 'A;' (Allow Access), and ';SY', which are all detected by the Sigma rule.
